# Codex Telegram Bridge v0.2.7

This release improves Telegram status fidelity and makes long-running Codex sessions easier to
scan from both channel posts and discussion messages.

## Highlights

- Filters closed sessions out of `/topics` and arranges its action buttons in a compact three-column
  layout consistent with the other command menus.
- Compresses subagent activity into short, single-line summaries with bounded task text and an
  aggregate count for completed agents.
- Keeps terminal Tasks counts, parent-agent status, channel and discussion moon phases, and
  deduplicated dashboard deliveries consistent across both Telegram targets.
- Clears recoverable `Reconnecting` state only after a newer healthy snapshot, while preserving a
  newer terminal error instead of incorrectly reporting recovery.
- Carries permission, approval, and sandbox settings through `/changemodel`; `/ask` remains an
  explicit read-only path.

## Upgrade notes

- Existing credentials, Telegram bindings, Codex state, session spaces, and installer-owned units
  are preserved by the v0.2.7 installer.
- The supported installer host remains Ubuntu or Debian under WSL2 with systemd and Python 3.14.
